title: diplacus rupicola
intro: Diplacus rupicola, also known as the mountain foxglove or rock bee orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. This plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Diplacus rupicola include alleviating coughs, relieving congestion, and reducing fever. The plant's medicinal properties can be utilized through various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent.
